title: Fix table/pair editing with inline images, consistent sizing, print-safe export, and updated theme colors from screenshots

requirements.text: Update the app theme to match the uploaded screenshots: make the home-screen navigation drawer/menu background white (not purple) and lighten the purple used across the UI to match the screenshots exactly (apply globally to buttons, borders, highlights, accents, and selected nav item).

quotes: Menu on home screen must be white and can you make purple less dark or like in my image

acceptanceCriteria: On mobile and desktop, the NavigationDrawer background is white and visually matches the uploaded screenshots.
Purple accents throughout the app (e.g., primary buttons, selected nav item, outlines) use the lighter purple matching the uploaded screenshots, not the current darker purple.
No regressions in dark-mode toggling (dark mode can remain purple-themed, but light mode must match the screenshots).

requirements.text: Fix table and match-pairs editing so users can click inside each cell/box and type independently without text being redirected to the main/top question text field.

quotes: not able to write anything in that box when I am selecting box of tablet or pair then it redirected to question before that table

acceptanceCriteria: When a table cell is clicked and the user types, only that cell content changes; the question text at the top does not change.
When a match-pairs box (Column A or Column B) is clicked and the user types, only that box content changes; the question text at the top does not change.
Typing into table/match-pairs cells works consistently on mobile and desktop.

requirements.text: Add inline image insertion inside table cells and match-pairs boxes supporting both upload-from-device and paste-from-clipboard, while allowing text and images within the same cell/box and ensuring nothing overflows outside the cell/box boundaries.

quotes: And nothing should go out of the box
And i also want option to insert image in that table or pair box

acceptanceCriteria: Each table cell and each match-pairs box provides a UI affordance to insert an image via file upload.
Pasting an image from the clipboard into a focused table cell/match-pairs box inserts the image into that cell/box.
Text can be entered in the same cell/box that contains an inserted image (both are persisted and rendered together).
Cell/box content is constrained: images and text do not visually overflow outside the cell/box boundary (overflow is clipped/wrapped appropriately).

requirements.text: For inserted images inside table/match-pairs cells, implement formatting controls: default size 5 × 5 cm (user adjustable), aspect ratio locked by default, alignment controls, and optional caption per image.
acceptanceCriteria: Newly inserted images default to 5cm × 5cm sizing in a print-relevant unit (cm) and render consistently in the editor and print preview.
Users can adjust the image size; by default, resizing preserves aspect ratio until the user explicitly unlocks it.
Users can set image alignment within the cell/box (e.g., left/center/right).
Users can add/edit/remove an image caption that stays associated with the image and renders under/near the image inside the same cell/box.

requirements.text: Enforce table sizing rules: all columns must have equal width across the table; within each row, all cells must share the same height (row-based equal heights), while different rows may have different heights.

quotes: Then width of 1,2,3,4,5,6 must be same and height of 1,2,3 must be same and height of 4,5,6 must be same  both height can be different

acceptanceCriteria: In the editor, a table with N columns renders with equal column widths regardless of per-cell content.
In the editor, all cells in a given row share the same height; rows can differ in height from other rows based on their content.
In print preview/printed output, the same equal-width and row-based height behavior is preserved as closely as the medium allows.

requirements.text: Ensure print-safe PDF rendering for tables/match-pairs containing inline images and captions, including proper wrapping/clipping behavior and predictable sizing for A4 print output.
acceptanceCriteria: Printing/export preview for papers containing table/match-pairs images does not cause images to overflow outside their cell/box in the printed output.
Tables and match-pairs content remain readable in print: text wraps and images scale within cell boundaries.
No print-only UI controls (buttons, toolbars) appear in the printed output.

requirements.text: Update stored paper/question data handling to persist the new richer table/match-pairs cell content (text + zero or more images with formatting metadata), including backward compatibility for existing saved papers in localStorage.

quotes: Table/pair editing fixes • Inline image insertion (upload + paste) • Text + image inside same cell

acceptanceCriteria: Existing saved papers that contain tableData.cells as string[][] continue to load without crashing, and are normalized into the new structure automatically.
After editing and reloading the app, table/match-pairs cell content (text, images, captions, size, alignment) is retained.
No runtime errors occur when rendering older or newer saved papers.

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose them instead).
Do not edit immutable hook files: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx.
Backend must remain single-actor; only add Motoko logic if truly required (this feature set should be frontend-only unless proven otherwise).
nonGoals: Implementing third-party image hosting/CDNs or external storage services for images.
Adding real-time collaboration or syncing across devices.
Introducing new authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
